concept_seed.one_liner: A lush specialty botanical garden and rare-plant nursery website with botanical-illustration headers, mossy greens and bone-white palette, plant encyclopedia pages, seasonal bloom calendar, garden map, workshop schedule, membership and shop pages, conservatory visit info, and editorial features on cultivars.

metadata.palette: moss green, bone white, soft coral

accepted_concept.site_goal: Position Fernwood Conservatory & Rare Plant Nursery as a destination garden and serious horticultural authority, while driving conservatory visits, plant purchases, workshop sign-ups, and patron memberships.
audience: Serious home gardeners and plant collectors
Patrons and members supporting plant conservation
accepted_concept.description: An editorial, herbarium-inspired site for Fernwood Conservatory, a 14-acre specialty botanical garden and rare-plant nursery in the Pacific Northwest. The visual system mimics a leather-bound Victorian botanical folio updated for the web: hand-pressed botanical illustration headers, ruled column lines, deckled-edge image plates, and italic latinate captions. Mossy green dominates with bone-white pages and soft coral accents for in-bloom callouts. Long-scrolling encyclopedia entries sit beside an interactive bloom calendar and a hand-drawn SVG garden map. The shop and membership flows feel curatorial rather than commercial.
accepted_concept.motif: Pressed-herbarium folio: deckled paper textures, taxonomic rule lines, hand-inked botanical illustration headers, hairline dividers, and italic Latin binomial captions over mossy green and bone white

pages.layout_pattern: Full-bleed illustrated hero plate over conservatory photo, followed by 'In Bloom This Week' coral-tagged carousel, a three-column editorial teaser grid, a mossy banded visit-planning strip, and a footer with herbarium-style index
sections: Illustrated hero with site name and tagline
In Bloom This Week carousel with month chip
Footer with garden hours, address, and indexed sitemap

pages.layout_pattern: Left filter rail with taxonomic facets, right grid of botanical-plate cards each with Latin binomial caption and conservation badge, alphabetical jump strip across the top
sections: Header with search input and alphabetical jump strip
Filter rail (family, hardiness zone, light, conservation status)

pages.layout_pattern: Two-column editorial layout: large illustration plate left, ruled prose right with sticky tab nav for Overview, Cultivation, Propagation, and In the Garden; related cultivars appear as small plates at the foot
sections: Quick facts ruled table (family, native range, hardiness, light, soil)
Sticky tabs (Overview / Cultivation / Propagation / In the Garden)

pages.layout_pattern: Centered circular SVG bloom wheel with month spokes and species arcs, month slider beneath, and a list view below showing currently peaking species with garden zone coordinates
sections: Heading with current month in italic Latin
Link to garden map for each species

pages.layout_pattern: Full-width interactive SVG map with mossy-green hand-drawn zones, right-rail zone detail panel that updates on hover or tap, legend along the bottom
sections: Map header with current bloom highlights toggle

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial month-view calendar at top, scrollable list of workshop cards below with instructor portrait roundels, skill-level chips, and remaining-seat badges
Filtered grid of nursery products with botanical-plate thumbnails, pot-size and provenance captions, soft coral 'Ships This Season' tags, and a left rail for filters
sections: Shop header with seasonal shipping notice
Filter rail (cultivar group, pot size, light, hardiness)

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial hero with patron testimonial, four-column tier table with ornament dividers, FAQ accordion below, and a footer band with patron honor roll names
sections: What your membership funds (conservation impact strip)

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial magazine grid with one large featured plate-essay and two-column secondary articles below, each tagged with cultivar or season, sidebar of recent dispatches
sections: Tag filter chips (cultivar, season, garden zone)

message_intent: Convey deep horticultural expertise and editorial seriousness
Invite physical visits to the glasshouses and grounds
Drive rare-plant nursery purchases and waitlist sign-ups
content_model: Cultivar encyclopedia entry (binomial name, common names, family, native range, hardiness, light/water/soil, propagation notes, conservation status, illustration plate, related species)
Bloom calendar event (species, peak window, garden zone, sun exposure, accompanying companions)
Garden zone (name, acreage, signature collection, accessibility notes, map coordinates)
Workshop session (title, instructor, date, duration, skill level, capacity, materials, price, member discount)
Editorial feature article (title, author, hero illustration, pull quotes, related cultivars)
Nursery product (cultivar, pot size, age, provenance, stock count, shipping season, price)
Membership tier (name, annual price, benefits list, guest passes, reciprocal gardens)
interactions: Month slider on bloom calendar that recolors a circular wheel of species in peak
Interactive SVG garden map with hover-highlighted zones and pinned cultivar markers
Encyclopedia search with filters for hardiness zone, light, family, and conservation status
Workshop calendar with month/list toggle and seat-count availability badges
Add-to-cart with seasonal shipping window notice and waitlist for sold-out cultivars
Sticky in-page tabs on cultivar pages (Overview / Cultivation / Propagation / In the Garden)
asset_needs: Hand-inked botanical illustrations for at least eight signature cultivars
Deckled-edge paper texture for plate frames
SVG hand-drawn garden map with labeled zones
Photography of glasshouses, beds, and workshop benches with warm overcast light
Botanical line-art ornaments and section dividers
accepted_concept.mobile_behavior: Single-column reflow with the garden map becoming a pan-and-zoom panel; encyclopedia tabs collapse to an accordion; bloom wheel becomes a horizontally scrollable month strip

strengths: Tight herbarium-folio motif carried into deckled plates, ruled columns, and italic binomial captions
Nine concrete pages directly covering seed asks: encyclopedia index + entry, bloom calendar, garden map, workshops, shop, membership, field notes
Specific, non-generic interactions (circular bloom wheel with month slider, SVG map with zone rail, sticky cultivar tabs, seasonal-shipping waitlist)
Rich content model with taxonomic facets, conservation status, propagation notes, and reciprocal-garden member benefits
Clear conservatory framing matches the seed's 'conservatory visit info' requirement
weaknesses: Encyclopedia entry path uses a single species and could feel templated if not varied
Membership page leans on a familiar tier-table pattern

strengths: Distinctive 'meridian' latitudinal-band organization is a fresh angle on a plant encyclopedia
Almanac week scrubber, postcode shipping check, and lineage drawers are concrete, useful interactions
Strong provenance and propagation content model that fits a small-batch nursery
Grower's Notebook gives a believable editorial channel separate from product pages
weaknesses: Drops the seed's membership page entirely and reframes 'conservatory' as a small glasshouse, weakening alignment
Workshops + Visit + About risk feeling like standard small-business pages compared to the more curatorial pages elsewhere
Almanac and encyclopedia-meridians could overlap conceptually if not carefully differentiated

issues.message: Concept declared the garden map at /visit/map, but the builder shipped it as garden-map.html at the root (with a separate visit.html for hours/admission). The intent — an interactive SVG garden map with right-rail zone detail, legend, and printed-map link — is fully satisfied by garden-map.html, so this is a route-naming difference rather than a missing page.

Three pages (Encyclopedia index, Field Notes, Shop) have unlabeled form controls — the encyclopedia search box, the Field Notes 'subscribe to the printed almanac' email input, and the Shop waitlist email input lack associated <label> elements or aria-label attributes.

On the Encyclopedia index, the metric flagged each cultivar plate card as 'fewer_than_two_children'. Visually the 12 cards are highly consistent (illustration, binomial, common name, family, badges); this looks like a structural-detection artifact, not a real layout problem.

Layout-consistency scores dip on the cultivar entry (45) and membership (58) pages. Both are intentionally editorial — two-column herbarium plate vs. ruled prose, tier table with varying internal benefit lists — so the asymmetry is by design and reads well in the screenshots.

repair_instructions: Add accessible labels to the three unlabeled form inputs: (1) in encyclopedia.html, give the cultivar search input either a visible <label> or an aria-label such as aria-label="Search the cultivar encyclopedia"; (2) in field-notes.html, label the 'Printed Almanac' subscribe email input (aria-label="Email address for the printed almanac"); (3) in shop.html, label the waitlist email input (aria-label="Email address for the nursery waitlist"). Keep the visual design unchanged.
